notes.anchor: a letter for Cassius arrived at Capua from Lucretius
notes.note: News reached Cicero second-hand: Lucretius (probably Q. Lucretius Vespillo, a Pompeian) wrote to the tribune C. Cassius at Capua, and Cicero reports the chain. The figures it carried punctured Philotimus's optimism of the day before.

notes.anchor: Domitius had not three thousand soldiers
notes.note: Against Philotimus's report of a strong army, Lucretius's letter put L. Domitius Ahenobarbus's real force at under three thousand men, a number too small to check Caesar; Domitius was indeed soon trapped at Corfinium.
